mentions.note: Debt and OpenAI-payment worries hit the stock; Cramer would buy on a bit more weakness.
Receiving hundreds of billions in OpenAI data center orders; directly exposed to OpenAI's funding success and revenue growth.

Down 3% in broad software sell-off. Proceed carefully until clarity emerges.
Don't like deteriorating balance sheet; even if it bounces, sell it—balance sheet quality critical screen.

Most important report this week; stock cut in half from $345 peak due to data center build-out debt concerns and OpenAI Texas project uncertainty; need clarity on profitability timeline and Cerner sale.

Good but others are better; Nvidia offers superior risk/reward despite massive pullback from highs.
Down 3.7% in software selloff; traditional enterprise software under pressure from hardware/AI trend.

Caller Jerry bought in 170s, looking to add on decline; Cramer says very tough to value, plunged into data center build at questionable time; don't like what developing away from Oracle; prefers to see June quarter first; wants to preserve capital.

Heavy debt load but 'fine' as a spring-load play on cloud/AI growth; beaten-down valuation offers leverage for those seeking it.
Up 93%, revenue +21%, massive debt load alongside CoreWeave; fine for leverage play but highly leveraged position.
